rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 16, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT MICHAEL NADELMAN'S Motion To Void The Judgment And For Leave To Defend The Action. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CLEANWELL COMPANY VS. JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion Of Pltf For Post Judgment Costs Pursuant To Ccp 685.080
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 16, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CLEANWELL COMPANY'S Motion For Post Judgment Costs Pursuant To CCP 685.080. Granted in part. Plaintiff is awarded an additional $10,000 as reasonable fees and costs. While plaintiff is entitled to an award of additional fees and costs, the amount sought is hugely excessive given the amount of the underlying judgment, the post-judgment work for which additional fees and costs are sought, and defendant's belated effort to satisfy the judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOSE PASCUAL et al VS. JIMMY DIAMOND GLASS COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Answers
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF JOSE PASCUAL Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS VS. POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 16, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANTS POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, LP, SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO I, LLC, GEORGE CRESSON, GREATER SONOMA PARTNERSHIP, LOANVEST I, LLC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Plaintiff acknowledges that the first, second, third and fourth causes of action are substantially re-pled from the Third Amended Complaint, and thus, notwithstanding the filing of the fourth amended complaint, in the interests of judicial economy and substantial justice, the court addresses the motion on the merits. Plaintiff has presented admissible evidence showing that there are triable issues whether he received the June 21, 2012 letter prior to his purported revocation of the offer of rescission and whether his dismissal of the tenth cause of action was effectively a revocation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BASS VS. JIANFENG CHEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of And Mtn For Order Compelling Pltf'S Deposition & Req. For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 4, DEFENDANT JIANFENG CHEN, 1021 YORK ST., LLC, KENNETH WEI, PATHMARK REALTY CORP Notice Of And Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff'S Deposition & Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Pos Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, with monetary sanctions awarded against plaintiff John Bass in the amount of $892.50 (assuming no reply briefing and no hearing). The Motion is granted on the grounds set forth in the moving papers, and the motion is not opposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE E. LOPEZ VS. VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 6, Vca Antech, Vca San Francisco Veterinary Specialists, Lp, Alan Stewart'S Motion To Compel Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, with monetary sanctions in the form of attorney's fees awarded against plaintiff Christine Lopez in the amount of $525. This figure represents 3 hours' time for estimated preparation of the instant motion (assuming no hearing). The motion is granted on the grounds set forth in the moving papers, and: (1) the Motion is not opposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KURT HAGGSTROM VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 16, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANT BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. No useful purpose is served by requiring plaintiff to amend. As to the first claim for breach of contract, liberally construed, at the very least, that claim adequately alleges that plaintiff is entitled to nominal damages. (CACI 3.60). The demurrer to the second claim for breach of the implied covenant is based on the assumption that the demurrer to the first claim will be sustained. As to the definition of the class, there is no doubt that plaintiff intended to define the class as excluding policies covered by ERISA. Defendant's request to take judicial notice is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
